name: Natanz Fuel Enrichment Plant
notes: Primary enrichment site; struck by US/Israel Operation Midnight Hammer June 2025

name: Fordow (FFEP)
notes: Underground enrichment plant near Qom; struck by B-2 bombers June 22 2025

name: Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center (INTC)
notes: Largest nuclear research complex; damaged by Israeli/US strikes June 2025

name: Isfahan Uranium Conversion Facility (UCF)
notes: Converts uranium ore to UF6 feed for enrichment

name: Arak IR-40 Research Reactor
notes: Redesigned under JCPOA to reduce weapons-grade Pu output

name: Tehran Nuclear Research Center (TNRC)
notes: 5 MW TRR research reactor; HEU fuel

name: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 1000 MWe VVER-1000; Russia-built civilian power reactor

name: Parchin Military Complex
notes: Suspected high-explosive testing for nuclear warhead development; IAEA denied access

name: Lavizan-Shian
notes: Site razed before IAEA inspection; suspected physics research

name: Yongbyon Nuclear Research Center
notes: Main nuclear complex; 5 MWe plutonium reactor, enrichment halls, reprocessing

name: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site
notes: All six DPRK nuclear tests conducted here; tunnels partially demolished 2018

name: Kangson Suspected Enrichment
notes: CSIS/38North identified as likely covert centrifuge site ca.2018

name: Kahuta (Khan Research Laboratories)
notes: Primary HEU enrichment; origin of A.Q. Khan network

name: Khushab Plutonium Production Reactors
notes: Four heavy water reactors producing weapons-grade plutonium

name: Karachi Nuclear Power Plant (KANUPP)
notes: CANDU-type; K-2 and K-3 new units under construction

name: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC)
notes: Dhruva 100 MW plutonium production reactor; reprocessing; weapons R&D

name: Madras Atomic Power Station (Kalpakkam)
notes: MAPS 1-2; also PFBR fast breeder under construction

name: Rattehalli Rare Materials Plant
notes: Uranium enrichment for naval reactor fuel

name: Negev Nuclear Research Center (Dimona)
notes: IRR-2 heavy water reactor; estimated 80-400 weapons; never declared to IAEA

name: Soreq Nuclear Research Center
notes: IRR-1 5 MW pool reactor under IAEA safeguards

name: Mayak Production Association (Ozersk)
notes: Plutonium production; reprocessing; 1957 Kyshtym disaster site

name: Novouralsk (Ural Electrochemical Combine)
notes: Largest uranium enrichment plant in Russia

name: Novaya Zemlya Nuclear Test Site
notes: World's largest nuclear test site; Tsar Bomba detonated here 1961

name: Leningrad NPP (Sosnovy Bor)
notes: RBMK + VVER-1200; close to St. Petersburg

name: Beloyarsk NPP
notes: BN-800 fast breeder + BN-1200 under construction

name: Balakovo NPP
notes: VVER-1000 x4; largest capacity NPP in Russia

name: Kursk NPP
notes: RBMK-1000; proximity to Ukraine front line

name: Akademik Lomonosov Floating NPP
notes: World's first floating NPP; Pevek, Arctic; 70 MWe

name: Jiuquan Atomic Energy Complex (Plant 404)
notes: First plutonium reactor; reprocessing; weapons material production; Gansu desert

name: Lop Nor Nuclear Test Site
notes: All 45 Chinese nuclear tests conducted here 1964-1996

name: CIAE Beijing (Nuclear Physics/Weapons R&D)
notes: China Institute of Atomic Energy; research reactor complex

name: Qinshan Nuclear Power Base
notes: Multiple units incl. first Chinese-built reactor; Zhejiang

name: Ling'ao Nuclear Power Station
notes: CPR-1000 units adjacent to Daya Bay

name: Tianwan Nuclear Power Station
notes: Russian VVER; Units 7-8 under construction; Jiangsu

name: Taishan Nuclear Power Station
notes: Two EPR units; world's first operational EPRs

name: Los Alamos National Laboratory
notes: Primary nuclear weapons design lab; Manhattan Project origin; W87 pit production

name: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
notes: Secondary weapons design lab; NIF laser fusion facility

name: Sandia National Laboratories
notes: Weapons engineering; non-nuclear components; Albuquerque + Livermore

name: Pantex Plant
notes: Primary US nuclear weapons assembly/disassembly; Amarillo TX

name: Y-12 National Security Complex
notes: HEU storage and processing; weapons secondary components; Oak Ridge TN

name: Savannah River Site
notes: Tritium production for warheads; HLW vitrification; Aiken SC

name: Hanford Site
notes: Former plutonium production; 56 million gallons HLW; largest US nuclear cleanup

name: Waste Isolation Pilot Plant (WIPP)
notes: Deep geological repository for transuranic defense waste; Carlsbad NM

name: Idaho National Laboratory
notes: Advanced reactor testing; spent fuel storage

name: Oak Ridge National Laboratory
notes: HFIR research reactor; isotope production; nuclear materials R&D

name: Palo Verde Nuclear Generating Station
notes: Largest NPP in US by output; 3 PWR units; Tonopah AZ

name: Vogtle Electric Generating Plant
notes: Units 3 & 4 first new US reactors in 30 years; AP1000

name: Diablo Canyon Power Plant
notes: California's last operating NPP; life extension approved 2023

name: Turkey Point Nuclear Plant
notes: Oldest operating NPP in US; Homestead FL

name: Salem / Hope Creek Generating Station
notes: 3 units (2 PWR + 1 BWR); Salem County NJ

name: Watts Bar Nuclear Plant
notes: 2 PWR; Rhea County TN; also tritium production

name: La Hague Reprocessing Plant
notes: World's largest civilian reprocessing plant; Normandy; handles foreign SNF

name: Marcoule Nuclear Center
notes: Plutonium weapons production (historical); MELOX MOX plant; G1 decommissioned

name: Tricastin Nuclear Power Center
notes: 4 PWR + Georges Besse II enrichment plant

name: Flamanville Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 2 PWR + EPR Unit 3 (first French EPR, connected 2024)

name: Gravelines Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 6 PWR; largest nuclear site in Western Europe by reactors

name: Sellafield
notes: World's most complex nuclear site; THORP reprocessing closed; legacy waste mgmt

name: AWE Aldermaston
notes: UK nuclear warhead design and manufacture

name: HMNB Clyde / Faslane and Coulport
notes: Trident submarine base; Coulport warhead storage; Scotland

name: Sizewell B Nuclear Power Station
notes: Only UK PWR; Suffolk; Sizewell C planned

name: Hinkley Point C Nuclear Power Station
notes: 2 EPR; Somerset; first UK NPP in decades

name: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant
notes: Europe's largest NPP; Russian-occupied since March 2022; 6 VVER-1000; multiple IAEA safety incidents

name: Chernobyl Exclusion Zone
notes: 1986 disaster site; RBMK-1000; sarcophagus + New Safe Confinement

name: Kori Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 4 PWR; oldest SK NPP; Unit 1 closed 2017

name: Wolsong (Wolseong) Nuclear Power Plant
notes: CANDU + 3 PHWR; Unit 1 closed; heavy water

name: Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 2011 tsunami disaster; ongoing decommissioning; ALPS treated water discharge started 2023

name: Kashiwazaki-Kariwa Nuclear Power Plant
notes: World's largest NPP by capacity; 7 BWR/ABWR; safety concerns

name: Rokkasho Reprocessing Plant
notes: Full-scale reprocessing; enrichment plant; MOX; long delayed

name: Chalk River Laboratories
notes: NRU decommissioning; ACPR; isotope production; Chalk River ON

name: Bruce Nuclear Generating Station
notes: 8 CANDU; world's largest operating nuclear facility by output

name: Doel Nuclear Power Station
notes: 4 PWR; Antwerp; Units 3&4 life extended to 2035

name: Olkiluoto Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 2 BWR + OL3 EPR (first European EPR in operation 2023)

name: Onkalo Spent Fuel Repository
notes: World's first deep geological repository for HLW; under construction

name: Beznau Nuclear Power Plant
notes: Oldest operating NPP in the world (1969); 2 PWR; Aargau

name: Temelín Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 2 VVER-1000; South Bohemia; 2 more units planned

name: Paks Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 4 VVER-440; only Hungarian NPP; Paks II (2 VVER-1200) under construction

name: Cernavodă Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 2 CANDU; Units 3-5 planned; only NPP in Romania

name: Kozloduy Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 2 VVER-1000 (4 older units closed); only Bulgarian NPP

name: Metsamor Nuclear Power Plant
notes: VVER-440; aging reactor in seismic zone; no containment; international safety concern

name: Ostrovets Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 2 VVER-1200; first NPP in Belarus; near Lithuania border

name: Barakah Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 4 APR-1400 (Korean); Arab world's first NPP; all units now operational

name: Akkuyu Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 4 VVER-1200; Turkey's first NPP; Mersin; Unit 1 under construction

name: El Dabaa Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 4 VVER-1200; Egypt's first NPP; north coast; construction started 2022

name: Rooppur Nuclear Power Plant
notes: 2 VVER-1200; Bangladesh's first NPP; Pabna district

name: Atucha Nuclear Power Plant
notes: Atucha 1 & 2 PHWR; only operational NPP in Southern Hemisphere (with Embalse)

name: Koeberg Nuclear Power Station
notes: 2 PWR; only NPP in Africa; Cape Town region

name: Borssele Nuclear Power Station
notes: 1 PWR; Zeeland; life extended to 2033+; 2nd unit planned

name: Semipalatinsk Nuclear Test Site
notes: 456 Soviet nuclear tests 1949-1989; ongoing remediation; legacy contamination

name: Mururoa Atoll Test Site
notes: 181 French nuclear tests 1966-1996; French Polynesia; subsidence monitoring

name: Maralinga Test Site
notes: 12 British nuclear tests 1956-1963; South Australia; partially remediated
